Architecture-first.description: Architecture variables move first, then partition, interface, package, and memory choices are projected afterward.

Cost-interface-first.description: Cost and interface projection is resolved early before package and memory closure.

Outer-loop repair.description: Outer-loop repair ordering kept as a strong projected baseline.

Joint.description: Joint search over the full PICASSO state tuple d=(m,a,k,i,p,b).

Memory-off.description: Projected control that disables memory-aware projection to quantify attachment sensitivity.

Package-oblivious.description: Projected baseline that ignores package richness during earlier stages and repairs package feasibility later.

Partition-first.description: Chiplet partitioning is fixed before architecture refinement and later package/interface closure.

Stage-wise.description: Stage-wise projected baseline that moves one variable family at a time with repair between stages.
